competitors.name: Calculator Soup
features: Clear formula explanation
Step-by-step calculation display
Multiple related calculators (percentage change, increase/decrease)
No ads on core calculator page
weaknesses: Outdated, dense UI
No interactive visualization
Mobile experience is functional but clunky
Limited real-world context or examples

competitors.name: Omni Calculator
features: Excellent, conversational explanations
Interactive input fields with live results
Clear distinction between difference and change
Strong SEO content with related use cases
weaknesses: Aggressive promotion of other calculators
Some pages have intrusive mid-content ads
Explanation can be verbose, burying the tool
Limited customization of output format

competitors.name: RapidTables
features: Extremely simple, fast-loading interface
Minimalist design focused purely on calculation
No-nonsense, immediate results
Part of a vast suite of quick calculators
weaknesses: Zero educational value or explanation
UI is stark and uninviting
No context for formula or interpretation
High ad density relative to tool simplicity

competitors.name: Percentage Calculator
features: Dedicated domain for high keyword relevance
Very simple two-field input
Quick result with formula shown
Links to a dozen other percentage calc types
weaknesses: Site is ad-heavy and feels spammy
Design is outdated and unprofessional
No depth, purely a transactional tool
Poor mobile ad placement disrupts use

competitors.name: GIGAcalculator
features: Clean, modern interface
Provides a practical interpretation of the result
Includes a useful difference vs. change guide
Offers downloadable results in CSV
weaknesses: Primary call-to-action is for other tools
Some features (like CSV) are under-promoted
Can be slow to load due to third-party scripts
Lacks visual comparison (e.g., a bar chart)

gaps.gap: Lack of integrated data visualization. No competitor shows the two values being compared in a simple bar or column chart alongside the numeric result.
gaps.implementation: Add a dynamic, minimal chart (using a library like Chart.js) that updates in real-time as users input values. Visually highlight the 'difference' as a gap or overlay.

gaps.gap: No scenario-based templates or examples for common use cases (e.g., 'Price Match Comparison', 'Salary Offer Difference', 'Test Score Improvement').
gaps.implementation: Create a dropdown menu with 4-5 common scenarios. Selecting one pre-fills the fields with labeled example data (e.g., 'Old Salary', 'New Salary') and provides a contextual interpretation sentence.

gaps.gap: Inability to save, share, or bookmark calculations. All tools are purely session-based.
gaps.implementation: Add a 'Share This Calculation' button that generates a unique URL with encoded inputs. Optionally, a simple 'Save as PDF/Image' function for reports.

gaps.gap: Poor handling of sequential or batch comparisons. Users cannot calculate the percentage difference between more than two numbers without starting over.
gaps.implementation: Allow users to add a third (or more) comparison field dynamically. The tool could show a matrix of differences or let the user compare each value to a baseline.

gaps.gap: Minimal focus on accessibility. None announce results to screen readers dynamically, and few have high-contrast modes or keyboard-navigation-first design.
gaps.implementation: Build with ARIA live regions for instant result announcement, ensure full keyboard operability, and include a high-contrast theme toggle.

recommended_positioning: The intuitive percentage difference calculator that helps you visualize comparisons and apply them to real-world decisions, not just get a number.
